original:
We made @camb_ai's paywall skippable.
if a user gets to the dashboard and sees voice dubbing in 140+ languages, real-time translation. and their own studio - the $14.99 sells itself.
It did, the conversions went up.

versions.label: A
versions.text:
We made @camb_ai's paywall skippable.

A user who reaches the dashboard sees voice dubbing in 140+ languages, real-time translation, their own studio. The $14.99 sells itself.

It did. Conversions went up.
versions.rationale: Minimal clean fix. Removes em-dash and broken sentence, fixes comma splice. Keeps the original rhythm and hook. Best for a design showcase caption.

versions.label: B
versions.text:
@camb_ai's conversions went up after we made the paywall skippable.

We moved the pricing gate. A user who reaches the dashboard first sees voice dubbing in 140+ languages, real-time translation, their own studio. By the upgrade screen, they've already decided.
versions.rationale: Result first, then mechanism. 'Moved the pricing gate' names the actual design decision (Rule 5). Ends on the reader concluding the $14.99 — doesn't state it (Rule 2).

versions.label: C
versions.text:
140 languages. Real-time translation. Your own studio. All before the paywall.

We moved @camb_ai's pricing gate to the end. Conversions went up.
versions.rationale: Tightest version. Features stack so 'all before the paywall' lands harder. Reader does all the work — no 'sells itself', no explanation. Just the result. The version no one else writes.
